top_categories.workout_context: 13,779ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
177ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo Collado del Condor's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
Gran Fondo Collado del Condor's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 77 miles.
At Gran Fondo Collado del Condor, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
13,779ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo Collado del Condor. Train it until it feels boring.
77 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Base fitness for Gran Fondo Collado del Condor. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Gran Fondo Collado del Condor, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 13,779ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo Collado del Condor’s 77 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Gran Fondo Collado del Condor with 13,779ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo Collado del Condor requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 77 miles of mixed terrain in Merida, Venezuela.
